NKJV.1: Now it came to pass, when Jesus finished commanding His twelve disciples, that He departed from there to teach and to preach in their cities.
NKJV.2: And when John had heard in prison about the works of Christ, he sent two of his disciples
NKJV.3: and said to Him, “Are You the Coming One, or do we look for another?”
NKJV.4: Jesus answered and said to them, “Go and tell John the things which you hear and see:
NKJV.5: The blind see and the lame walk; the lepers are cleansed and the deaf hear; the dead are raised up and the poor have the gospel preached to them.
NKJV.6: And blessed is he who is not  offended because of Me.”
NKJV.7: As they departed, Jesus began to say to the multitudes concerning John: “What did you go out into the wilderness to see?  A reed shaken by the wind?
NKJV.8: But what did you go out to see? A man clothed in soft garments? Indeed, those who wear soft clothing are in kings’ houses.
NKJV.9: But what did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I say to you,  and more than a prophet.
NKJV.10: For this is he of whom it is written: ‘Behold, I send My messenger before Your face, Who will prepare Your way before You.’
NKJV.11: “Assuredly, I say to you, among those born of women there has not risen one greater than John the Baptist; but he who is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.
NKJV.12: And from the days of John the Baptist until now the kingdom of heaven suffers violence, and the violent take it by force.
NKJV.13: For all the prophets and the law prophesied until John.
NKJV.14: And if you are willing to receive it, he is Elijah who is to come.
NKJV.15: He who has ears to hear, let him hear!
NKJV.16: “But to what shall I liken this generation? It is like children sitting in the marketplaces and calling to their companions,
NKJV.17: and saying: ‘We played the flute for you, And you did not dance; We mourned to you, And you did not lament.’
NKJV.18: For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, ‘He has a demon.’
NKJV.19: The Son of Man came eating and drinking, and they say, ‘Look, a glutton and a winebibber,  a friend of tax collectors and sinners!’  But wisdom is justified by her children.”
NKJV.20: Then He began to rebuke the cities in which most of His mighty works had been done, because they did not repent:
NKJV.21: “Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the mighty works which were done in you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago  in sackcloth and ashes.
NKJV.22: But I say to you,  it will be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon in the day of judgment than for you.
NKJV.23: And you, Capernaum,  who are exalted to heaven, will be brought down to Hades; for if the mighty works which were done in you had been done in Sodom, it would have remained until this day.
NKJV.24: But I say to you  that it shall be more tolerable for the land of Sodom in the day of judgment than for you.”
NKJV.25: At that time Jesus answered and said, “I thank You, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, that You have hidden these things from the wise and prudent and have revealed them to babes.
NKJV.26: Even so, Father, for so it seemed good in Your sight.
NKJV.27: All things have been delivered to Me by My Father, and no one knows the Son except the Father. Nor does anyone know the Father except the Son, and the one to whom the Son wills to reveal Him.
NKJV.28: Come to Me, all you who labor and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest.
NKJV.29: Take My yoke upon you  and learn from Me, for I am gentle and  lowly in heart,  and you will find rest for your souls.
NKJV.30: For My yoke is easy and My burden is light.”

ESV.1: When Jesus had finished instructing his twelve disciples, he went on from there to teach and preach in their cities.
ESV.2: Now when John heard in prison about the deeds of the Christ, he sent word by his disciples
ESV.3: and said to him, “Are you the one who is to come, or shall we look for another?”
ESV.4: And Jesus answered them, “Go and tell John what you hear and see:
ESV.5: the blind receive their sight and the lame walk, lepers  are cleansed and the deaf hear, and the dead are raised up, and the poor have good news preached to them.
ESV.6: And blessed is the one who is not offended by me.”
ESV.7: As they went away, Jesus began to speak to the crowds concerning John: “What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A reed shaken by the wind?
ESV.8: What then did you go out to see? A man  dressed in soft clothing? Behold, those who wear soft clothing are in kings' houses.
ESV.9: What then did you go out to see? A prophet?  Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
ESV.10: This is he of whom it is written,  “‘Behold, I send my messenger before your face, who will prepare your way before you.’
ESV.11: Truly, I say to you, among those born of women there has arisen no one greater than John the Baptist. Yet the one who is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.
ESV.12: From the days of John the Baptist until now the kingdom of heaven has suffered violence,  and the violent take it by force.
ESV.13: For all the Prophets and the Law prophesied until John,
ESV.14: and if you are willing to accept it, he is Elijah who is to come.
ESV.15: He who has ears to hear,  let him hear.
ESV.16: “But to what shall I compare this generation? It is like children sitting in the marketplaces and calling to their playmates,
ESV.17: “‘We played the flute for you, and you did not dance; we sang a dirge, and you did not mourn.’
ESV.18: For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, ‘He has a demon.’
ESV.19: The Son of Man came eating and drinking, and they say, ‘Look at him! A glutton and a drunkard, a friend of tax collectors and sinners!’ Yet wisdom is justified by her deeds.”
ESV.20: Then he began to denounce the cities where most of his mighty works had been done, because they did not repent.
ESV.21: “Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the mighty works done in you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago in sackcloth and ashes.
ESV.22: But I tell you, it will be more bearable on the day of judgment for Tyre and Sidon than for you.
ESV.23: And you, Capernaum, will you be exalted to heaven? You will be brought down to Hades. For if the mighty works done in you had been done in Sodom, it would have remained until this day.
ESV.24: But I tell you that it will be more tolerable on the day of judgment for the land of Sodom than for you.”
ESV.25: At that time Jesus declared, “I thank you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, that you have hidden these things from the wise and understanding and revealed them to little children;
ESV.26: yes, Father, for such was your gracious will.
ESV.27: All things have been handed over to me by my Father, and no one knows the Son except the Father, and no one knows the Father except the Son and anyone to whom the Son chooses to reveal him.
ESV.28: Come to me, all who labor and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest.
ESV.29: Take my yoke upon you, and learn from me, for I am gentle and lowly in heart, and you will find rest for your souls.
ESV.30: For my yoke is easy, and my burden is light.”

NASB.1: When Jesus had finished giving instructions to His twelve disciples, He departed from there to teach and preach in their cities.
NASB.2: Now when John, while imprisoned, heard of the works of Christ, he sent [word] by his disciples
NASB.3: and said to Him, 'Are You the Expected One, or shall we look for someone else?'
NASB.4: Jesus answered and said to them, 'Go and report to John what you hear and see:
NASB.5: [the] BLIND RECEIVE SIGHT and [the] lame walk, [the] lepers are cleansed and [the] deaf hear, [the] dead are raised up, and [the] POOR HAVE THE GOSPEL PREACHED TO THEM.
NASB.6: 'And blessed is he who does not take offense at Me.'
NASB.7: As these men were going [away], Jesus began to speak to the crowds about John, 'What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A reed shaken by the wind?
NASB.8: 'But what did you go out to see? A man dressed in soft [clothing]? Those who wear soft [clothing] are in kings' palaces!
NASB.9: 'But what did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and one who is more than a prophet.
NASB.10: 'This is the one about whom it is written, 'BEHOLD, I SEND MY MESSENGER AHEAD OF YOU, WHO WILL PREPARE YOUR WAY BEFORE YOU.'
NASB.11: 'Truly I say to you, among those born of women there has not arisen [anyone] greater than John the Baptist! Yet the one who is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.
NASB.12: 'From the days of John the Baptist until now the kingdom of heaven suffers violence, and violent men take it by force.
NASB.13: 'For all the prophets and the Law prophesied until John.
NASB.14: 'And if you are willing to accept [it], John himself is Elijah who was to come.
NASB.15: 'He who has ears to hear, let him hear.
NASB.16: 'But to what shall I compare this generation? It is like children sitting in the market places, who call out to the other [children],
NASB.17: and say, 'We played the flute for you, and you did not dance; we sang a dirge, and you did not mourn.'
NASB.18: 'For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, 'He has a demon!'
NASB.19: 'The Son of Man came eating and drinking, and they say, 'Behold, a gluttonous man and a drunkard, a friend of tax collectors and sinners!' Yet wisdom is vindicated by her deeds.'
NASB.20: Then He began to denounce the cities in which most of His miracles were done, because they did not repent.
NASB.21: 'Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the miracles had occurred in Tyre and Sidon which occurred in you, they would have repented long ago in sackcloth and ashes.
NASB.22: 'Nevertheless I say to you, it will be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon in [the] day of judgment than for you.
NASB.23: 'And you, Capernaum, will not be exalted to heaven, will you? You will descend to Hades; for if the miracles had occurred in Sodom which occurred in you, it would have remained to this day.
NASB.24: 'Nevertheless I say to you that it will be more tolerable for the land of Sodom in [the] day of judgment, than for you.'
NASB.25: At that time Jesus said, 'I praise You, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, that You have hidden these things from [the] wise and intelligent and have revealed them to infants.
NASB.26: 'Yes, Father, for this way was well-pleasing in Your sight.
NASB.27: 'All things have been handed over to Me by My Father; and no one knows the Son except the Father; nor does anyone know the Father except the Son, and anyone to whom the Son wills to reveal [Him].
NASB.28: 'Come to Me, all who are weary and heavy-laden, and I will give you rest.
NASB.29: 'Take My yoke upon you and learn from Me, for I am gentle and humble in heart, and YOU WILL FIND REST FOR YOUR SOULS.
NASB.30: 'For My yoke is easy and My burden is light.'

NLT.1: When Jesus had finished giving these instructions to his twelve disciples, he went out to teach and preach in towns throughout the region.
NLT.2: John the Baptist, who was in prison, heard about all the things the Messiah was doing. So he sent his disciples to ask Jesus,
NLT.3: “Are you the Messiah we’ve been expecting, or should we keep looking for someone else?”
NLT.4: Jesus told them, “Go back to John and tell him what you have heard and seen —
NLT.5: the blind see, the lame walk, those with leprosy are cured, the deaf hear, the dead are raised to life, and the Good News is being preached to the poor.”
NLT.6: And he added, “God blesses those who do not fall away because of me. ”
NLT.7: As John’s disciples were leaving, Jesus began talking about him to the crowds. “What kind of man did you go into the wilderness to see? Was he a weak reed, swayed by every breath of wind?
NLT.8: Or were you expecting to see a man dressed in expensive clothes? No, people with expensive clothes live in palaces.
NLT.9: Were you looking for a prophet? Yes, and he is more than a prophet.
NLT.10:
John is the man to whom the Scriptures refer when they say, ‘Look, I am sending my messenger ahead of you,
and he will prepare your way before you.’
NLT.11: “I tell you the truth, of all who have ever lived, none is greater than John the Baptist. Yet even the least person in the Kingdom of Heaven is greater than he is!
NLT.12: And from the time John the Baptist began preaching until now, the Kingdom of Heaven has been forcefully advancing, and violent people are attacking it.
NLT.13: For before John came, all the prophets and the law of Moses looked forward to this present time.
NLT.14: And if you are willing to accept what I say, he is Elijah, the one the prophets said would come.
NLT.15: Anyone with ears to hear should listen and understand!
NLT.16: “To what can I compare this generation? It is like children playing a game in the public square. They complain to their friends,
NLT.17:
‘We played wedding songs,
and you didn’t dance,
so we played funeral songs,
and you didn’t mourn.’
NLT.18: For John didn’t spend his time eating and drinking, and you say, ‘He’s possessed by a demon.’
NLT.19: The Son of Man, on the other hand, feasts and drinks, and you say, ‘He’s a glutton and a drunkard, and a friend of tax collectors and other sinners!’ But wisdom is shown to be right by its results.”
NLT.20: Then Jesus began to denounce the towns where he had done so many of his miracles, because they hadn’t repented of their sins and turned to God.
NLT.21: “What sorrow awaits you, Korazin and Bethsaida! For if the miracles I did in you had been done in wicked Tyre and Sidon, their people would have repented of their sins long ago, clothing themselves in burlap and throwing ashes on their heads to show their remorse.
NLT.22: I tell you, Tyre and Sidon will be better off on judgment day than you.
NLT.23: “And you people of Capernaum, will you be honored in heaven? No, you will go down to the place of the dead. For if the miracles I did for you had been done in wicked Sodom, it would still be here today.
NLT.24: I tell you, even Sodom will be better off on judgment day than you.”
NLT.25: At that time Jesus prayed this prayer: “O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, thank you for hiding these things from those who think themselves wise and clever, and for revealing them to the childlike.
NLT.26: Yes, Father, it pleased you to do it this way!
NLT.27: “My Father has entrusted everything to me. No one truly knows the Son except the Father, and no one truly knows the Father except the Son and those to whom the Son chooses to reveal him.”
NLT.28: Then Jesus said, “Come to me, all of you who are weary and carry heavy burdens, and I will give you rest.
NLT.29: Take my yoke upon you. Let me teach you, because I am humble and gentle at heart, and you will find rest for your souls.
NLT.30: For my yoke is easy to bear, and the burden I give you is light.”

WEB.1: When Jesus had finished directing his twelve disciples, he departed from there to teach and preach in their cities.
WEB.2: Now when John heard in the prison the works of Christ, he sent two of his disciples
WEB.3: and said to him, “Are you he who comes, or should we look for another?”
WEB.4: Jesus answered them, “Go and tell John the things which you hear and see:
WEB.5: the blind receive their sight, the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, the deaf hear,  the dead are raised up, and the poor have good news preached to them.
WEB.6: Blessed is he who finds no occasion for stumbling in me.”
WEB.7: As these went their way, Jesus began to say to the multitudes concerning John, “What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A reed shaken by the wind?
WEB.8: But what did you go out to see? A man in soft clothing? Behold, those who wear soft clothing are in kings’ houses.
WEB.9: But why did you go out? To see a prophet? Yes, I tell you, and much more than a prophet.
WEB.10: For this is he, of whom it is written, ‘Behold, I send my messenger before your face, who will prepare your way before you.’
WEB.11: Most certainly I tell you, among those who are born of women there has not arisen anyone greater than John the Baptizer; yet he who is least in the Kingdom of Heaven is greater than he.
WEB.12: From the days of John the Baptizer until now, the Kingdom of Heaven suffers violence, and the violent take it by force.
WEB.13: For all the prophets and the law prophesied until John.
WEB.14: If you are willing to receive it, this is Elijah, who is to come.
WEB.15: He who has ears to hear, let him hear.
WEB.16: “But to what shall I compare this generation? It is like children sitting in the marketplaces, who call to their companions
WEB.17: and say, ‘We played the flute for you, and you didn’t dance. We mourned for you, and you didn’t lament.’
WEB.18: For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, ‘He has a demon.’
WEB.19: The Son of Man came eating and drinking, and they say, ‘Behold, a gluttonous man and a drunkard, a friend of tax collectors and sinners!’ But wisdom is justified by her children.”
WEB.20: Then he began to denounce the cities in which most of his mighty works had been done, because they didn’t repent.
WEB.21: “Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the mighty works had been done in Tyre and Sidon which were done in you, they would have repented long ago in sackcloth and ashes.
WEB.22: But I tell you, it will be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon on the day of judgment than for you.
WEB.23: You, Capernaum, who are exalted to heaven, you will go down to Hades.   For if the mighty works had been done in Sodom which were done in you, it would have remained until today.
WEB.24: But I tell you that it will be more tolerable for the land of Sodom, on the day of judgment, than for you.”
WEB.25: At that time, Jesus answered, “I thank you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, that you hid these things from the wise and understanding, and revealed them to infants.
WEB.26: Yes, Father, for so it was well-pleasing in your sight.
WEB.27: All things have been delivered to me by my Father. No one knows the Son, except the Father; neither does anyone know the Father, except the Son, and he to whom the Son desires to reveal him.
WEB.28: “Come to me, all you who labor and are heavily burdened, and I will give you rest.
WEB.29: Take my yoke upon you, and learn from me, for I am gentle and humble in heart; and you will find rest for your souls.
WEB.30: For my yoke is easy, and my burden is light.”

CSB17.1: When Jesus had finished giving instructions to his twelve disciples, he moved on from there to teach and preach in their towns. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.2: Now when John heard in prison what the Christ was doing, he sent a message through his disciples <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.3: and asked him, “Are you the one who is to come, or should we expect someone else?” <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.4: Jesus replied to them, “Go and report to John what you hear and see:
CSB17.5: The blind receive their sight, the lame walk, those with leprosy <sup>[1]</sup>are cleansed, the deaf hear, the dead are raised, and the poor are told the good news, <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.6: and blessed is the one who isn’t offended by me.” <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.7: As these men were leaving, Jesus began to speak to the crowds about John: “What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A reed swaying in the wind? <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.8: What then did you go out to see? A man dressed in soft clothes? See, those who wear soft clothes are in royal palaces.
CSB17.9: What then did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.10:
This is the one about whom it is written: See, I am sending my messenger ahead of you;
he will prepare your way before you. [2] ⓗ
CSB17.11: “Truly I tell you, among those born of women no one greater than John the Baptist has appeared, <sup>[3]</sup> but the least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.
CSB17.12: From the days of John the Baptist until now, the kingdom of heaven has been suffering violence, <sup>[4]</sup>and the violent have been seizing it by force.
CSB17.13: For all the prophets and the law prophesied until John.
CSB17.14: And if you’re willing to accept it, he is the Elijah who is to come. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.16: “To what should I compare this generation? It’s like children sitting in the marketplaces who call out to other children:
CSB17.17:
We played the flute for you,
but you didn’t dance;
we sang a lament,
but you didn’t mourn! [6]
CSB17.18: For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, ‘He has a demon!’ <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.19: The Son of Man came eating and drinking, and they say, ‘Look, a glutton and a drunkard, <sup>ⓛ</sup>a friend of tax collectors and sinners!’ <sup>ⓜ</sup>Yet wisdom is vindicated <sup>[7]</sup>by her deeds.” <sup>[8]</sup>
CSB17.20: Then he proceeded to denounce the towns where most of his miracles were done, because they did not repent:
CSB17.21: “Woe to you, Chorazin! <sup>ⓝ</sup>Woe to you, Bethsaida! <sup>ⓐ</sup>For if the miracles that were done in you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, <sup>ⓑ</sup>they would have repented in sackcloth and ashes long ago.
CSB17.22: But I tell you, it will be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon on the day of judgment <sup>ⓒ</sup>than for you.
CSB17.23: And you, Capernaum, will you be exalted to heaven? No, you will go down to Hades. <sup>ⓓ</sup>For if the miracles that were done in you had been done in Sodom, it would have remained until today.
CSB17.24: But I tell you, it will be more tolerable for the land of Sodom on the day of judgment than for you.”
CSB17.25: At that time Jesus said, <sup>ⓔ</sup> “I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because you have hidden these things from the wise and intelligent and revealed them to infants. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.26: Yes, Father, because this was your good pleasure. <sup>[9]</sup>
CSB17.27: All things have been entrusted to me by my Father. No one knows the Son except the Father, and no one knows the Father except the Son and anyone to whom the Son desires <sup>[10]</sup> to reveal him. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.28: “Come to me, all of you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.29: Take up my yoke and learn from me, <sup>ⓘ</sup>because I am lowly and humble in heart, and you will find rest for your souls. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.30: For my yoke is easy and my burden is light.”

AMP.1: When Jesus had finished giving instructions to His twelve disciples, He went on from there to teach and to preach in their [Galilean] cities.
AMP.2: Now when John [the Baptist] in prison heard about the activities of Christ, he sent word by his disciples
AMP.3: and asked Him, "Are You the Expected One (the Messiah), or should we look for someone else [who will be the promised One]?"
AMP.4: Jesus answered, "Go and report to John what you hear and see:
AMP.5: the blind receive [their] sight and the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed [by healing] and the deaf hear, the dead are raised, and the poor have the gospel preached to them.
AMP.6: And blessed [joyful, favored by God] is he who does not take offense at Me [accepting Me as the Messiah and trusting confidently in My message of salvation]."
AMP.7: As these men were going away, Jesus began to speak to the crowds about John: "What did you go out in the wilderness to see? A reed shaken by the wind [which is commonplace]?
AMP.8: What did you go out to see? A man dressed in soft clothing [entirely unsuited for the harsh desert]? Those who wear soft clothing are in the palaces of kings!
AMP.9: But what did you [really] go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and one [more eminent, more remarkable, and] far more than a prophet [who foretells the future].
AMP.10:
This is the one of whom it is written [by the prophet Malachi],
'Behold, I send My messenger ahead of You,
Who will prepare Your way before You.'
AMP.11: I assure you and most solemnly say to you, among those born of women there has not risen anyone greater than John the Baptist; yet the one who is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater [in privilege] than he.
AMP.12: From the days of John the Baptist until now the kingdom of heaven suffers violent assault, and violent men seize it by force [as a precious prize].
AMP.13: For all the prophets and the Law prophesied up until John.
AMP.14: And if you are willing to accept it, John himself is [the fulfillment of] Elijah [as the messenger] who was to come [before the kingdom].
AMP.15: He who has ears to hear, let him hear and heed My words.
AMP.16: "But to what shall I compare this generation? It is like little children sitting in the market places, who call to the others,
AMP.17: and say 'We piped the flute for you [playing wedding], and you did not dance; we wailed sad dirges [playing funeral], and you did not mourn and cry aloud.'
AMP.18: For John came neither eating nor drinking [with others], and they say, 'He has a demon!'
AMP.19: The Son of Man came eating and drinking [with others], and they say, 'Look! A glutton and a drunkard, a friend of tax collectors and sinners [including non-observant Jews]!' Yet wisdom is justified and vindicated by her deeds [in the lives of those who respond to Me]."
AMP.20: Then He began to denounce [the people in] the cities in which most of His miracles were done, because they did not repent [and change their hearts and lives].
AMP.21: "Woe (judgment is coming) to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the miracles done in you had been done in Tyre and Sidon [cities of the Gentiles], they would have repented long ago in sackcloth and ashes [their hearts would have been changed and they would have expressed sorrow for their sin and rebellion against God].
AMP.22: Nevertheless I say to you, it will be more tolerable for [the pagan cities of] Tyre and Sidon on the day of judgment than for you.
AMP.23: And you, Capernaum, are you to be exalted to heaven [for your apathy and unresponsiveness]? You will descend to Hades (the realm of the dead); for if the miracles done in you had been done in Sodom, it would have remained until this day.
AMP.24: But I say to you, it will be more tolerable for the land of Sodom on the day of judgment, than for you."
AMP.25: At that time Jesus said, "I praise You, Father, Lord of heaven and earth [I openly and joyfully acknowledge Your great wisdom], that You have hidden these things [these spiritual truths] from the wise and intelligent and revealed them to infants [to new believers, to those seeking God's will and purpose].
AMP.26: Yes, Father, for this way was well-pleasing in Your sight.
AMP.27: All things have been handed over to Me by My Father; and no one fully knows and accurately understands the Son except the Father; and no one fully knows and accurately understands the Father except the Son, and anyone to whom the Son [deliberately] wills to reveal Him.
AMP.28: "Come to Me, all who are weary and heavily burdened [by religious rituals that provide no peace], and I will give you rest [refreshing your souls with salvation].
AMP.29: Take My yoke upon you and learn from Me [following Me as My disciple], for I am gentle and humble in heart, and you will find rest (renewal, blessed quiet) for your souls.
AMP.30: For My yoke is easy [to bear] and My burden is light."

MSG.1: When Jesus finished placing this charge before his twelve disciples, he went on to teach and preach in their villages.
MSG.2: John, meanwhile, had been locked up in prison. When he got wind of what Jesus was doing, he sent his own disciples
MSG.3: to ask, "Are you the One we've been expecting, or are we still waiting?"
MSG.4: Jesus told them, "Go back and tell John what's going on:
MSG.5: The blind see, The lame walk, Lepers are cleansed, The deaf hear, The dead are raised, The wretched of the earth learn that God is on their side.
MSG.6: "Is this what you were expecting? Then count yourselves most blessed!"
MSG.7: When John's disciples left to report, Jesus started talking to the crowd about John. "What did you expect when you went out to see him in the wild? A weekend camper?
MSG.8: Hardly. What then? A sheik in silk pajamas? Not in the wilderness, not by a long shot.
MSG.9: What then? A prophet? That's right, a prophet! Probably the best prophet you'll ever hear.
MSG.10: He is the prophet that Malachi announced when he wrote, 'I'm sending my prophet ahead of you, to make the road smooth for you.'
MSG.11: "Let me tell you what's going on here: No one in history surpasses John the Baptizer; but in the kingdom he prepared you for, the lowliest person is ahead of him.
MSG.12: For a long time now people have tried to force themselves into God's kingdom.
MSG.13: But if you read the books of the Prophets and God's Law closely, you will see them culminate in John, teaming up with him in preparing the way for the Messiah of the kingdom.
MSG.14: Looked at in this way, John is the 'Elijah' you've all been expecting to arrive and introduce the Messiah.
MSG.15: "Are you listening to me? Really listening?
MSG.16: "How can I account for this generation? The people have been like spoiled children whining to their parents,
MSG.17: 'We wanted to skip rope, and you were always too tired; we wanted to talk, but you were always too busy.'
MSG.18: John came fasting and they called him crazy.
MSG.19: I came feasting and they called me a lush, a friend of the riff-raff. Opinion polls don't count for much, do they? The proof of the pudding is in the eating."
MSG.20: Next Jesus let fly on the cities where he had worked the hardest but whose people had responded the least, shrugging their shoulders and going their own way.
MSG.21: "Doom to you, Chorazin! Doom, Bethsaida! If Tyre and Sidon had seen half of the powerful miracles you have seen, they would have been on their knees in a minute.
MSG.22: At Judgment Day they'll get off easy compared to you.
MSG.23: And Capernaum! With all your peacock strutting, you are going to end up in the abyss. If the people of Sodom had had your chances, the city would still be around.
MSG.24: At Judgment Day they'll get off easy compared to you."
MSG.25: Abruptly Jesus broke into prayer: "Thank you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth. You've concealed your ways from sophisticates and know-it-alls, but spelled them out clearly to ordinary people.
MSG.26: Yes, Father, that's the way you like to work."
MSG.27: Jesus resumed talking to the people, but now tenderly. "The Father has given me all these things to do and say. This is a unique Father-Son operation, coming out of Father and Son intimacies and knowledge. No one knows the Son the way the Father does, nor the Father the way the Son does. But I'm not keeping it to myself; I'm ready to go over it line by line with anyone willing to listen.
MSG.28: "Are you tired? Worn out? Burned out on religion? Come to me. Get away with me and you'll recover your life. I'll show you how to take a real rest.
MSG.29: Walk with me and work with me—watch how I do it. Learn the unforced rhythms of grace. I won't lay anything heavy or ill-fitting on you.
MSG.30: Keep company with me and you'll learn to live freely and lightly."

NIV.1:
Jesus and John the Baptist
After Jesus had finished instructing his twelve disciples, he went on from there to teach and preach in the towns of Galilee.
NIV.2: When John heard in prison what Christ was doing, he sent his disciples
NIV.3: to ask him, “Are you the one who was to come, or should we expect someone else?”
NIV.4: Jesus replied, “Go back and report to John what you hear and see:
NIV.5: The blind receive sight, the lame walk, those who have leprosy are cured, the deaf hear, the dead are raised, and the good news is preached to the poor.
NIV.6: Blessed is the man who does not fall away on account of me.”
NIV.7: As John’s disciples were leaving, Jesus began to speak to the crowd about John: “What did you go out into the desert to see? A reed swayed by the wind?
NIV.8: If not, what did you go out to see? A man dressed in fine clothes? No, those who wear fine clothes are in kings’ palaces.
NIV.9: Then what did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
NIV.10:
This is the one about whom it is written:
“‘I will send my messenger ahead of you,
who will prepare your way before you.’
NIV.11: I tell you the truth: Among those born of women there has not risen anyone greater than John the Baptist; yet he who is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.
NIV.12: From the days of John the Baptist until now, the kingdom of heaven has been forcefully advancing, and forceful men lay hold of it.
NIV.13: For all the Prophets and the Law prophesied until John.
NIV.14: And if you are willing to accept it, he is the Elijah who was to come.
NIV.15: He who has ears, let him hear.
NIV.16: “To what can I compare this generation? They are like children sitting in the marketplaces and calling out to others:
NIV.17:
“‘We played the flute for you,
and you did not dance;
we sang a dirge,
and you did not mourn.’
NIV.18: For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, ‘He has a demon.’
NIV.19: The Son of Man came eating and drinking, and they say, ‘Here is a glutton and a drunkard, a friend of tax collectors and “sinners.”’ But wisdom is proved right by her actions.”
NIV.20:
Woe on Unrepentant Cities
Then Jesus began to denounce the cities in which most of his miracles had been performed, because they did not repent.
NIV.21: “Woe to you, Korazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! If the miracles that were performed in you had been performed in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago in sackcloth and ashes.
NIV.22: But I tell you, it will be more bearable for Tyre and Sidon on the day of judgment than for you.
NIV.23: And you, Capernaum, will you be lifted up to the skies? No, you will go down to the depths. If the miracles that were performed in you had been performed in Sodom, it would have remained to this day.
NIV.24: But I tell you that it will be more bearable for Sodom on the day of judgment than for you.”
NIV.25:
Rest for the Weary
At that time Jesus said, “I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because you have hidden these things from the wise and learned, and revealed them to little children.
NIV.26: Yes, Father, for this was your good pleasure.
NIV.27: “All things have been committed to me by my Father. No one knows the Son except the Father, and no one knows the Father except the Son and those to whom the Son chooses to reveal him.
NIV.28: “Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest.
NIV.29: Take my yoke upon you and learn from me, for I am gentle and humble in heart, and you will find rest for your souls.
NIV.30: For my yoke is easy and my burden is light.”

NRSVCE.3: and said to him, “Are you the one who is to come, or are we to wait for another?”

This is the one about whom it is written, ‘See, I am sending my messenger ahead of you,
who will prepare your way before you.’

NRSVCE.13: For all the prophets and the law prophesied until John came;
NRSVCE.14: and if you are willing to accept it, he is Elijah who is to come.
NRSVCE.16: “But to what will I compare this generation? It is like children sitting in the marketplaces and calling to one another,
NRSVCE.17:
‘We played the flute for you, and you did not dance;
we wailed, and you did not mourn.’
NRSVCE.18: For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, ‘He has a demon’;
NRSVCE.19: the Son of Man came eating and drinking, and they say, ‘Look, a glutton and a drunkard, a friend of tax collectors and sinners!’ Yet wisdom is vindicated by her deeds.”
NRSVCE.20: Then he began to reproach the cities in which most of his deeds of power had been done, because they did not repent.
NRSVCE.21: “Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the deeds of power done in you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago in sackcloth and ashes.
NRSVCE.22: But I tell you, on the day of judgment it will be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon than for you.
NRSVCE.23:
And you, Capernaum, will you be exalted to heaven?
No, you will be brought down to Hades.
For if the deeds of power done in you had been done in Sodom, it would have remained until this day.
NRSVCE.24: But I tell you that on the day of judgment it will be more tolerable for the land of Sodom than for you.”
NRSVCE.25: At that time Jesus said, “I thank you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because you have hidden these things from the wise and the intelligent and have revealed them to infants;
NRSVCE.26: yes, Father, for such was your gracious will.
NRSVCE.27: All things have been handed over to me by my Father; and no one knows the Son except the Father, and no one knows the Father except the Son and anyone to whom the Son chooses to reveal him.
NRSVCE.28: “Come to me, all you that are weary and are carrying heavy burdens, and I will give you rest.
NRSVCE.29: Take my yoke upon you, and learn from me; for I am gentle and humble in heart, and you will find rest for your souls.
NRSVCE.30: For my yoke is easy, and my burden is light.”

NET.1: When Jesus had finished instructing his twelve disciples, he went on from there to teach and preach in their towns.
NET.2: Now when John heard in prison about the deeds Christ had done, he sent his disciples to ask a question:
NET.3: “Are you the one who is to come, or should we look for another?”
NET.4: Jesus answered them, “Go tell John what you hear and see:
NET.5: The blind see, the lame walk, lepers are cleansed, the deaf hear, the dead are raised, and the poor have good news proclaimed to them.
NET.6: Blessed is anyone who takes no offense at me.”
NET.7: While they were going away, Jesus began to speak to the crowd about John: “What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A reed shaken by the wind?
NET.8: What did you go out to see? A man dressed in fancy clothes? Look, those who wear fancy clothes are in the homes of kings!
NET.9: What did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
NET.10: This is the one about whom it is written: ‘Look, I am sending my messenger ahead of you, who will prepare your way before you.’
NET.11: “I tell you the truth, among those born of women, no one has arisen greater than John the Baptist. Yet the one who is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he is.
NET.12: From the days of John the Baptist until now the kingdom of heaven has suffered violence, and forceful people lay hold of it.
NET.13: For all the prophets and the law prophesied until John appeared.
NET.14: And if you are willing to accept it, he is Elijah, who is to come.
NET.15: The one who has ears had better listen!
NET.16: “To what should I compare this generation? They are like children sitting in the marketplaces who call out to one another,
NET.17: ‘We played the flute for you, yet you did not dance; we wailed in mourning, yet you did not weep.’
NET.18: For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, ‘He has a demon!’
NET.19: The Son of Man came eating and drinking, and they say, ‘Look at him, a glutton and a drunk, a friend of tax collectors and sinners!’ But wisdom is vindicated by her deeds.”
NET.20: Then Jesus began to criticize openly the cities in which he had done many of his miracles, because they did not repent.
NET.21: “Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! If the miracles done in you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago in sackcloth and ashes.
NET.22: But I tell you, it will be more bearable for Tyre and Sidon on the day of judgment than for you!
NET.23: And you, Capernaum, will you be exalted to heaven? No, you will be thrown down to Hades! For if the miracles done among you had been done in Sodom, it would have continued to this day.
NET.24: But I tell you, it will be more bearable for the region of Sodom on the day of judgment than for you!”
NET.25: At that time Jesus said, “I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because you have hidden these things from the wise and intelligent, and revealed them to little children.
NET.26: Yes, Father, for this was your gracious will.
NET.27: All things have been handed over to me by my Father. No one knows the Son except the Father, and no one knows the Father except the Son and anyone to whom the Son decides to reveal him.
NET.28: Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest.
NET.29: Take my yoke on you and learn from me, because I am gentle and humble in heart, and you will find rest for your souls.
NET.30: For my yoke is easy to bear, and my load is not hard to carry.”
